name: Arcipreste de Hita Trail
name_original: Ciudad Encantada de Tamajón — bucle trailrunning desde el embalse de el Vado
description: This difficult 9.5-mile (15.3 km) jogging trail features runnable tracks and a steep climb to the Peña del Arcipreste.
region_intro: Jogging around Retiendas offers diverse terrain within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park and the Sierra de Ayllón region of Spain. The area features the Jarama River, which carves impressive gorges, and varied topography ranging from lush forests to rugged landscapes. Joggers can explore paths with different elevation gains, including unique rock formations and routes near waterfalls and lagoons. This region provides a natural setting for running, combining exercise with exploration of its distinct geographical features. Best jogging routes around Retiendas The most popular jogging route is Arcipreste de Hita Trail, a 9.5 miles (15.3 km) trail that takes 2 hours 0 minutes to complete. This route passes through the natural park, offering varied scenery. Another top favourite among local runners is Aljibe Pools Circular Trail, a difficult 6.9 miles (11.1 km) path. This route features significant elevation changes and natural pools. Local runners also love the Aljibe Waterfall – Trillos Bridge loop from Campillo de Ranas, a 5.6 miles (9.0 km) trail leading through the Jarama River gorge and past a waterfall, often completed in about 1 hour 14 minutes. Jogging around Retiendas is defined by river gorges, varied forest terrain, and unique rock formations. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate paths to more challenging trails with significant inclines. The routes in Retiendas are highly rated by the komoot community. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Retiendas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running trails around Retiendas?
A: The region around Retiendas, nestled within the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park, offers a diverse range of terrain. You'll find paths alongside the &lt;strong&gt;Jarama River&lt;/strong&gt;, carving impressive gorges, as well as routes through lush forests and more open, rugged landscapes. The area also features unique rock formations, sometimes referred to as an 'Enchanted City', providing varied scenery for your run.

Q: Are there any challenging running routes for experienced runners in Retiendas?
A: Yes, Retiendas offers several challenging routes with significant elevation changes. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030895550/aljibe-pools-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Pools Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) path featuring natural pools and notable ascents. Another demanding option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541180" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Ocejón loop from Almiruete&lt;/a&gt;, which is 11.6 miles (18.6 km) long with over 1000 meters of elevation gain.

Q: Can I find running routes that pass by waterfalls or other natural landmarks?
A: Absolutely. The region is known for its beautiful natural features. You can explore routes that lead past captivating waterfalls like El Chorro de Valdesotos or the stunning Lagunas de Puebla de Beleña. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Waterfall – Trillos Bridge loop from Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 5.6-mile (9.0 km) trail that takes you through the Jarama River gorge and past a waterfall.

Q: Are there any circular jogging routes available in Retiendas?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. A notable option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1030895550/aljibe-pools-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Pools Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a difficult 6.9-mile (11.1 km) loop. Another is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143011" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aljibe Waterfall – Trillos Bridge loop from Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt;, which is a 5.6-mile (9.0 km) circular trail.

Q: What historical or cultural sights can I see while running in Retiendas?
A: You can combine your run with exploration of historical sites. A popular path leads from Retiendas to the impressive 12th-century &lt;strong&gt;Monasterio de Bonaval&lt;/strong&gt;. You might also pass through or near traditional 'black architecture' villages like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323305" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roblelacasa&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt;, characterized by buildings made with dark slate and stone, adding a cultural dimension to your outdoor activity.

Q: Are there any family-friendly running trails in the Retiendas area?
A: While many routes in Retiendas are rated as difficult, there are moderate options that could be suitable for families looking for a less strenuous run. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20142993" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hillside Trail – El Aljibe Waterfall loop from Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.3-mile (6.9 km) route, offering a good balance of scenery and accessibility. Always check the route details for specific elevation and terrain to ensure it suits your family's fitness level.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running routes in Retiendas?
A: The Sierra Norte de Guadalajara Natural Park, where Retiendas is located, generally welcomes dogs on trails. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in protected areas or near wildlife, and to carry water for both yourself and your pet. Please respect local regulations and ensure your dog does not disturb other users or wildlife.

Q: Where can I park my car when accessing the running trails in Retiendas?
A: Specific parking information for each trailhead can vary. However, villages like Retiendas itself, or nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4157355" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Campillo de Ranas&lt;/a&gt;, often have designated parking areas or street parking available. It's advisable to check the starting point of your chosen route on komoot for more precise parking recommendations.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Retiendas?
A: The varied topography and natural beauty of Retiendas make it suitable for running throughout much of the year. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes, with lush vegetation or beautiful fall colors. Summer mornings and evenings can also be ideal to avoid the midday heat, especially when running alongside the shaded Jarama River gorges. Winters can be cooler but offer crisp air and unique scenery.

Q: How many running routes are available around Retiendas?
A: There are over 15 running routes available around Retiendas on komoot, offering a wide range of distances and difficulties. This includes routes like the popular &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1031390028/arcipreste-de-hita-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Arcipreste de Hita Trail&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.5-mile (15.3 km) route that passes through the natural park.

Q: Are there any viewpoints or scenic spots along the running trails?
A: Many running routes in Retiendas offer stunning viewpoints and scenic spots. The area's varied topography means you'll encounter panoramic views of the Sierra Norte de Guadalajara. The 'Hoces del Jarama' gorges provide impressive canyon-like vistas, and the unique rock formations near the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323339" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hermitage of Nuestra Señora de Los Enebrales&lt;/a&gt; offer intriguing sights during your run.
tips: The primitive church was built in the 13th century and was Romanesque. It was enlarged in the 16th century by order of the Mendoza family, already in a Renaissance style. It is well preserved, so it is a perfect example to realize the characteristic color that the Tamajón stone takes.

Limestone rock formation with caves, cavities in the rock, rock arches, sinkholes and other formations. Thus, among the rocks and their capricious shapes you will be able to find small caves, bridges to cross and many strange shapes.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Arcipreste de Hita Trail?
faqs.answer: The main route, as mapped on komoot, covers approximately 15.3 kilometers and can typically be jogged in about 2 hours. However, if you opt for the full 20-kilometer circuit mentioned in some descriptions, especially with the challenging ascent to Peña del Arcipreste, it could take between 2 to 3 hours depending on your pace and fitness level.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Arcipreste de Hita Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail primarily features a wide, rolling track known as the 'pista de la Molinera,' which is comfortable and runnable for most of its length. However, be prepared for a significant change about one kilometer before reaching the N-VI, where a more vertical trail branches off towards the Peña del Arcipreste. This section involves a strenuous, albeit short, climb.

faqs.question: Is the Arcipreste de Hita Trail suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: While much of the trail is 'rodadora' (runnable) and generally comfortable, the overall difficulty is rated as 'difficult' due to its length and a notable strenuous climb to the Peña del Arcipreste. Beginners with good fitness might manage, but those new to trail jogging should be aware of the challenging ascent and consider their endurance for the full distance.

faqs.question: What points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The trail offers several notable sights. You'll pass by the Church of Our Lady of the Assumption and the unique rock formations of the Enchanted City of Tamajón. A major highlight, and the trail's namesake, is the Peña del Arcipreste, a natural monument reached via a steep detour. You'll also enjoy views of the 'solana de La Peñota' (the sunny side of La Peñota mountain).

faqs.question: Are there water sources available on the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, there are several reliable fountains along the route. You can find the Fuente de la Peñota around kilometer 4.2 and Fuente La Piedra around kilometer 7.9, providing convenient spots to refill your water bottle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be enjoyed in various seasons, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures and beautiful scenery. Summer can be hot, especially on the 'solana de La Peñota,' so an early start is recommended. Winter jogging is possible but be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially icy or snowy conditions, especially at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Arcipreste de Hita Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Arcipreste de Hita Trail is generally dog-friendly. However, as it passes through natural areas, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure the safety and enjoyment of all trail users. Be sure to carry water for your dog, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to access the trail?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need a specific permit or to pay an entrance fee to jog the Arcipreste de Hita Trail. It is freely accessible to the public. However, please be mindful that the trail is located within the Parque Natural de la Sierra Norte de Guadalajara, so always adhere to general park regulations regarding waste, noise, and environmental protection.

faqs.question: Where are the main parking and access points for the Arcipreste de Hita Trail?
faqs.answer: The trail is often accessed from the town of Cercedilla. While specific parking areas aren't detailed, Cercedilla serves as a common starting point with various parking options available within or near the town, providing convenient access to the trail network.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several other official trails. Approximately 72% of the Arcipreste de Hita Trail follows the 'Camino del Arcipreste de Hita.' It also includes smaller segments of the 'Pueblos de la Arquitectura Negra (Etapa 4)' (14%), 'Pueblos de la Arquitectura Negra (Etapa 3)' (12%), and the 'BTT 3 Ciudad Encantada' (8%).

faqs.question: What's an insider tip for tackling the steep ascent to Peña del Arcipreste?
faqs.answer: The detour to Peña del Arcipreste is a short but very steep climb, often described as 15 minutes of 'heavy breathing.' To make this challenging section more manageable, consider conserving energy on the preceding 'pista de la Molinera.' Don't be afraid to power hike or walk this segment; it's a rewarding effort for the views and the sense of accomplishment at the natural monument.

waypoints.name: Church of Our Lady of the Assumption
